title: Ready, Aim, Marry Me

jokes.text: What tipped you? The falling profits... or that we're a regular feature on Bill O'Reilly's 'Most Ridiculous Item of the Day'?
jokes.setup: Lucille says she thinks the company's in trouble
jokes.punchline: Michael sarcastically lists the obvious signs they're already famously failing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael deadpan sarcasm with specific pop culture reference. Economy is excellent - delivers multiple levels of failure in one line.

jokes.text: that bitch! You're not at home, Mom. She doesn't live next door when you're here.
jokes.setup: Lucille calls Lucille 2 a bitch
jokes.punchline: Michael points out she's talking loudly about someone who doesn't live nearby
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's practical nature vs Lucille's dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: the joke's on her, because she doesn't know how little I care for Gob. Think that makes the joke on Gob.
jokes.setup: Lucille thinks she's clever because Lucille 2 doesn't know how little she cares for Gob
jokes.punchline: Michael points out this actually makes Gob the victim
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark family dynamic - Lucille is so awful she doesn't realize she's insulting her own son. Michael's correction is perfectly logical yet devastating.

jokes.text: Does that mean she's gonna have to come live with us? No, no. No. It was- It was her drink
jokes.setup: George Michael sees evidence of an 'accident' and assumes the worst
jokes.punchline: Michael's panicked clarification that it was just spilled alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on family dynamics and George Michael's innocent assumptions.

jokes.text: I look like Edgar Winter. Forget it. I gotta get to spin class.
jokes.setup: Stan Sitwell has very white/blonde hair
jokes.punchline: He compares himself to famously albino musician Edgar Winter
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag with specific pop culture reference. Self-deprecating humor from a recurring character.

jokes.text: What's Kama Sutra oil? Maybe it's not for us.
jokes.setup: Father and son going through romantic date package together
jokes.punchline: George Michael innocently asks about sexual product, Michael realizes the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy from the innocent son asking about adult content while father tries to bond.

jokes.text: Well, yes, but I'm afraid I prematurely shot my wad on what was supposed to be a dry run, if you will. So now I'm afraid I have something of a mess on my hands.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining he went to check out the date package first
jokes.punchline: String of accidental sexual innuendo in innocent context
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias accidental innuendo - multiple sexual references in completely innocent context. Brilliant character-specific comedy that became a running gag template.

jokes.text: It's just-There's so many poorly chosen words in that sentence.
jokes.setup: Tobias's unintentionally sexual statement
jokes.punchline: Michael directly acknowledges the absurdity to the audience
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary that acknowledges the show's awareness of its own absurdity. Michael as straight man calling out the comedy.

jokes.text: You just did.
jokes.setup: Tobias says 'I'll take it' about the romantic package
jokes.punchline: Clerk points out he literally just took/consumed the body chocolate
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay pun with good timing. Not character-specific but well-executed.

jokes.text: [Screeching] 'Where's Tobias?' [Normal Voice] 'Oh, he's just sharing a romantic horseback ride with Michael Bluth... and they're dipping each other in-'
jokes.setup: Tobias imagining Lindsay being jealous
jokes.punchline: The absurd romantic scenario between two straight men
jokes.explanation: Tobias's complete obliviousness to how this sounds, plus his voice work makes this memorable

jokes.text: Oh, he's just sharing a romantic horseback ride with Michael Bluth and they're dipping each other in—
jokes.setup: Tobias fantasizing about making Lindsay jealous
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates homoerotic scenario with Michael
jokes.explanation: More Tobias accidental innuendo, this time creating awkward romantic scenario with his brother-in-law.

jokes.text: I tell you, you can zing your arrow into my buttocks anytime.
jokes.setup: Michael offers to play Cupid
jokes.punchline: Tobias responds with unintentionally sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias sexual innuendo in response to innocent Cupid reference. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Okay. You know what you do? Buy yourself a tape recorder. Record yourself for a whole day. I think you're gonna be surprised at some of your phrasing.
jokes.setup: Tobias's continued sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperated suggestion to make Tobias aware of his word choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the episode's tag sequence. Meta-joke about the character's verbal tics that pays off later.

jokes.text: Chaw-chee-chaw, chee-chaw. What is that? Is that a chicken?
jokes.setup: Michael is supposedly afraid to ask out Sally
jokes.punchline: Lindsay makes bizarre chicken noises as insult, Michael confused by the sound choice
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with the weird chicken sound. Michael's confused reaction adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: You too. This is great. Come on. These aren't even birds.
jokes.setup: Both Lindsay and Gob are making chicken noises at Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael points out the logical flaw in their mockery
jokes.explanation: Michael's logical objection to their nonsensical animal noises. Good escalation and character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm playing the part of the gigolo so that she'll keep the company afloat.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his relationship with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: He frames prostituting himself as noble sacrifice for the family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - reframing his selfish/opportunistic behavior as heroic sacrifice. Dark family dynamics.

jokes.text: She thinks you're a loser. 'Cause she knows you're a loser.
jokes.setup: Michael asks why Sally would avoid him and why Lucille 2 would be distant
jokes.punchline: Lindsay gives the same blunt answer to both questions
jokes.explanation: Good repetition structure with Lindsay's characteristic bluntness. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm— [bleep] Lucille 2. Oh. I could do something like that.
jokes.setup: Discussion about family members not contributing
jokes.punchline: Gob accidentally reveals his sexual relationship, Lindsay considers similar approach
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Gob's lack of filter and Lindsay's opportunistic nature.

jokes.text: George Sr. was in a tight spot... as he'd gotten himself wedged under the furnace while chasing after a sour ball
jokes.setup: Narrator says George Sr. is 'in a tight spot' suggesting business trouble
jokes.punchline: Literal tight spot - wedged under furnace chasing candy
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator misdirection - metaphor becomes literal reality. Visual absurdity of grown man stuck chasing candy.

jokes.text: this may just be me having my head against a hundred-degree furnace for an hour
jokes.setup: George Sr. about to give business advice
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his compromised mental state
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan acknowledgment of his ridiculous situation while still trying to maintain authority

jokes.text: But the audience was not ready to accept Bullet as a romantic lead... despite his powerful upper body.
jokes.setup: Jack Dorso tried to make solo movies
jokes.punchline: Narrator's deadpan assessment of why romance didn't work despite obvious qualification
jokes.explanation: Deadpan narrator humor suggesting physical fitness was the only romantic qualification attempted.

jokes.text: Release! [Cracking] Too much!
jokes.setup: Jack attempts birthday strength feat
jokes.punchline: Catastrophic failure that permanently disables him
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy - the moment that created the character's current condition. Good buildup and payoff.

jokes.text: See if you can talk your mother into taking another bullet for the Bluth family.
jokes.setup: George Sr. suggesting Lucille help with Jack Dorso
jokes.punchline: References both Jack's character name 'Bullet' and metaphorical sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning - literal reference to Jack's character name and metaphorical 'taking a bullet' for family. Perfect wordplay with dark undertones.

jokes.text: Nice of you to take a break from that common whore you've been dating.
jokes.setup: Lucille greeting Buster
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults his girlfriend in trademark harsh style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - no pleasantries, straight to cruel judgment. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: But sometimes love should be terrifying.
jokes.setup: Buster defending his experienced girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His twisted romantic philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster philosophy - takes healthy relationship advice to neurotic extreme. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'll be right in, and then maybe we can talk about— Makin' that love.
jokes.setup: Buster talking to his girlfriend through door
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism delivered with inappropriate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Buster's awkward attempt at being sexual. The pause and emphasis make it perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: She's kind, and she's caring... and she's certainly less experienced than that whore that I'm dating now.
jokes.setup: Buster defending Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults his current girlfriend while praising his ex
jokes.explanation: Buster's innocent honesty creates perfect irony - defending someone by insulting someone else.

jokes.text: Someone order 140 pounds of upper-body strength?
jokes.setup: Jack Dorso arriving at the house
jokes.punchline: Announces himself like a pizza delivery with oddly specific measurement
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction that immediately establishes his obsession with his remaining strength.

jokes.text: [Shouting] Walk, Dragon! Walk! - Enter the room!
jokes.setup: Jack arriving with his assistant Dragon
jokes.punchline: Jack shouting commands like Dragon is a pet
jokes.explanation: Jack's commanding personality and Dragon's name creating absurd pet-like dynamic

jokes.text: He's half deaf. It was a stupid, stupid hire. Not 'higher.' Eye level! Eye level!
jokes.setup: Dragon (Jack's assistant) can't hear properly
jokes.punchline: Lucille's frustrated commentary and Jack's continued misdirection
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with hearing impairment creating confusion. Lucille's blunt assessment is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I was never in the army. I was in the pictures. Here comes Uncle Jack. Shoot me!
jokes.setup: Jack confused about Buster being army man
jokes.punchline: References his movie career with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - brings back his movie persona and catchphrase.

jokes.text: I was never in the army. I was in the pictures. Here comes UncleJack. Shoot me!
jokes.setup: Jack meeting Buster in army uniform
jokes.punchline: Jack's catchphrase from his old radio/film career
jokes.explanation: Character consistency - Jack still performing his old character's catchphrase. The 'pictures' phrasing shows his era.

jokes.text: Back to the blonde. Back to the blonde. [Makes Gobbling Noise]
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Lucille's appearance
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate turkey/gobbling sound while being sexual
jokes.explanation: Creepy old man behavior with bizarre animal sound. Character-appropriate but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: Just ignore it! Ignore it. Just something the body does when you're shaken.
jokes.setup: Jack making gurgling sounds when shaken
jokes.punchline: His casual explanation of disturbing bodily function
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with his matter-of-fact acceptance of his body's limitations.

jokes.text: which would be a drag. Yeah, that's just the kind of joke Drag can't hear.
jokes.setup: Lindsay makes pun about going with 'Drag' (Dragon) instead of Tobias
jokes.punchline: Michael points out the deaf assistant can't hear her wordplay
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the pun itself, plus callback to Dragon's hearing impairment. Well-constructed.

jokes.text: So, I didn't even fail, and I don't see you giving me credit for that.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Lindsay for not even trying
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's twisted logic seeking credit for not failing because she didn't try
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted logic that's perfectly Lindsay. Absurd rationalization delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: particularly when that sister of yours started giving me the cutie eye. She did?
jokes.setup: Jack claims Lindsay was flirting with him
jokes.punchline: Michael's surprised/concerned reaction
jokes.explanation: Sets up the uncomfortable situation. Michael's reaction shows his realization of what he's getting into.

jokes.text: I won't go wheelchair! Twirl me around.
jokes.setup: Jack discussing his mobility limitations
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration followed by immediate request to be spun like a child
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing his stubborn pride immediately undercut by childlike request.

jokes.text: The business meant the world to him, but his sister's honor— uh, meant a lot too. Let's do it.
jokes.setup: Narrator describes Michael's moral dilemma
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately chooses business over sister's honor
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Michael's priorities. The pause and quick decision make it perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: He said he'd like to sleep on it.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks what Jack said about the money
jokes.punchline: Michael's euphemism for the sexual arrangement
jokes.explanation: Double meaning works well - innocent business phrase with sexual undertone.

jokes.text: In her robe and slippies? Getting into her head.
jokes.setup: Buster asking why Gob is at Lucille 2's
jokes.punchline: Gob's ridiculous excuse for being in her intimate wear
jokes.explanation: Gob's attempt at spy sophistication while obviously in an intimate situation

jokes.text: These kind of skills? Not really.
jokes.setup: Buster asks if Gob needs his stealth skills
jokes.punchline: Gob's honest assessment after seeing Buster's demonstration
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Buster's pride in his army training.

jokes.text: You were pimping out my mother. Yes, because she was sleeping with my brother
jokes.setup: Michael defends pimping out Lindsay
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s matter-of-fact justification
jokes.explanation: Dark family dynamics where incest makes prostitution seem reasonable. Perfect Bluth family logic.

jokes.text: I just screwed my brother-in-law. Well, I'm all grown up now.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes he's betrayed Tobias
jokes.punchline: George Michael misinterprets as literal sexual act and responds maturely
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with George Michael's innocent misunderstanding and mature response to what he thinks is adult confession.

jokes.text: Stop. You dropped the boot. Dropped the boot.
jokes.setup: Lindsay arriving for the romantic date
jokes.punchline: Jack's obsessive focus on the dropped boot rather than the woman
jokes.explanation: Character-specific obsessive behavior. Shows his controlling nature in small details.

jokes.text: Well, you certainly didn't help with my reputation as a lady's man with Jeff.
jokes.setup: Tobias on romantic getaway with Michael instead of Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His concern about his reputation with the horse handler
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - completely oblivious to how this looks but concerned about his 'reputation' with strangers.

jokes.text: Hey, did you ever get that tape recorder? You're funny. Come on, boy. Hoo-cha-cha!
jokes.setup: Michael references his earlier suggestion about Tobias recording himself
jokes.punchline: Tobias dismisses it while immediately making another questionable statement
jokes.explanation: Good callback that immediately pays off with another questionable Tobias phrase. Sets up the episode's tag.

jokes.text: thejets proved too powerful for UncleJack's tiny legs
jokes.setup: Jack in the spa portion of the romantic package
jokes.punchline: His legs are too small to control the water jets
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy playing on his physical limitations in an unexpected way.

jokes.text: Do you have any idea how often you say the word 'afraid'?
jokes.setup: Tobias suggesting Michael needs the tape recorder
jokes.punchline: Points out Michael's verbal tic while missing his own
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Tobias criticizing someone else's word choice while being completely oblivious to his own issues.

jokes.text: But we lost her. Everyone put on white robes and outsmarted us.
jokes.setup: Gob and Buster were following Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Gob's explanation of how they were foiled by standard spa attire
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating normal spa dress code as elaborate disguise scheme. Perfect Gob overcomplication.

jokes.text: How'd you like to take a nap, baldy? I'll handle this.
jokes.setup: Confrontation building at the spa
jokes.punchline: Jack's old-fashioned tough guy threat followed by Gob's overconfidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with period-appropriate tough guy language from Jack and typical Gob bravado.

jokes.text: She's not with him. She's with the Bullet. Oh, thank God.
jokes.setup: Tobias thinks Lindsay is with Stan Sitwell
jokes.punchline: Relief when he learns she's with the disabled movie character instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Tobias's relief at the 'better' option shows how bad Sitwell must be.

jokes.text: But if you want a fight, you got it. Shoot me!
jokes.setup: Confrontation escalating
jokes.punchline: Jack uses his character's catchphrase as a real fighting challenge
jokes.explanation: Good callback using his established catchphrase in actual conflict. Shows how he blends fiction and reality.

jokes.text: It was pretty obvious when Dragon kept swooping UncleJack in for a kiss.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining she knew it wasn't a real date
jokes.punchline: The romantic gestures were between Jack and his assistant, not her
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy reveal - the 'romantic' date was actually between the two men.

jokes.text: I had no idea a 90-year-old man could cave in my chest cavity like that.
jokes.setup: Tobias fought Jack in the spa
jokes.punchline: His surprised assessment of Jack's remaining strength
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy aftermath. Tobias's clinical description of his own injury is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I think this duct tape is the only thing that's keeping the ankle on. Okay. Ah. Right around there. Oh. I think you're right inside me now.
jokes.setup: Michael examining Tobias's injury
jokes.punchline: Tobias's inadvertently sexual description of medical examination
jokes.explanation: Perfect final Tobias accidental innuendo. Even while injured, he can't help making sexual references.

jokes.text: Even if it means me taking a chubby, I will suck it up. Nothing wrong with that.
jokes.setup: Tobias listens to his recorded words per Michael's suggestion
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo he doesn't recognize, followed by defensive dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's setup. Tobias still doesn't get it even when hearing it back. Brilliant character consistency.

jokes.text: I wouldn't mind kissing that man between the cheeks, so to speak.
jokes.setup: More of Tobias's recorded words
jokes.punchline: Extremely sexual statement delivered as innocent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Even more outrageous than the previous example. The 'so to speak' makes it worse by suggesting awareness he clearly lacks.

jokes.text: Tobias, you blowhard!
jokes.setup: Tobias finally realizes something is wrong with his speech
jokes.punchline: Uses another inadvertently sexual term while calling himself out
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect ending - Tobias finally gets it but immediately does it again. The word 'blowhard' is perfectly chosen. Comedy perfection.

tmdb_overview: With the company in dire financial straits and the Bluths no longer the primary shareholders, Michael must turn to old family friend "Uncle" Jack Dorso for financial assistance. Uncle Jack, however, wants something in return.
insight: Ninety-four-point Humor Index built on relentless character comedy and absurdist chaos—pure density.
